tips: Quickly scan the full paragraph first — context activates your vocabulary.
Think about word stems and suffixes (e.g., -ly, -tion, -ing, -ity).
Ask yourself: is the missing word a noun, verb, adjective, or adverb?
If you're stuck, make your best guess and move on — don't waste time.
Words come from academic vocabulary — studying the Academic Word List helps.
The missing letters are always in the second half of the word.
whatToExpect: One or two paragraphs of about 70 words each.
Ten words per paragraph have missing letters in their second half.
You type the missing letters into blank fields inline.
The topic is usually academic but not overly technical.
You get about 3–4 minutes per passage.
